name: Black Bean Chili Over Sweet Potatoes
recipeIngredient: 1 can (14.5 to 16 ounces) Ready-cut tomatoes with juices
1 can (15 to 16 ounces) Black beans, rinsed thoroughly and drained
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 400°F. Place sweet potatoes in a baking dish. Pierce the potatoes with a fork and bake until tender, about 1 hour and 15 minutes.
2. Meanwhile, heat olive oil in a large non-stick skillet over medium-high heat. Add diced bell pepper and chopped onions and sauté until golden, about 10 minutes.
3. Add minced garlic and stir the mixture for 2 minutes. Stir in chili powder and ground cumin, then add tomatoes with juices and beans, bringing the mixture to a simmer. Reduce heat to low, cover, and cook for 20 minutes. (The chili can be prepared up to one day ahead. Cover and refrigerate. Reheat over low heat before proceeding.)
4. Add diced squash and minced jalapeno chili; cover and cook until the squash is crisp-tender, about 6 minutes.
5. Arrange one sweet potato on each plate. Split the potatoes open slightly and mash them gently. Spoon some chili over each sweet potato. Squeeze lime juice over and top with yogurt (or sour cream) and chopped parsley if desired. Pass any remaining chili separately.
